question:
I'm new to this codebase and need a real code-based tour before my first behavior change.

Find the central runtime flow, trace it from the public API to the code that does the work, and show the nearby code paths and tests I should read next.

graph.answer:
The central runtime starts in [lua/lualine.lua](C:\Users\samch\AppData\Local\Temp\samchon-graph-corpus\lualine\lua\lualine.lua#L680). `setup()` is the entrypoint; it normalizes config, rebuilds highlight groups, loads all components/extensions, installs the Neovim options and autocmds, and starts the refresh timer/checker. From there, `statusline`, `tabline`, and `winbar` are the exported render entrypoints, while `refresh()` is the side-effecting updater that decides what to redraw and writes the actual `statusline`/`tabline`/`winbar` options.

**Runtime Flow**
1. Public API surface: [lua/lualine.lua](C:\Users\samch\AppData\Local\Temp\samchon-graph-corpus\lualine\lua\lualine.lua#L701) exports `setup`, `statusline`, `tabline`, `winbar`, `refresh`, `hide`, and `get_config`. If you want the real call path, start at `setup()` around [line 680](C:\Users\samch\AppData\Local\Temp\samchon-graph-corpus\lualine\lua\lualine.lua#L680).
2. Config normalization: [lua/lualine/config.lua](C:\Users\samch\AppData\Local\Temp\samchon-graph-corpus\lualine\lua\lualine\config.lua#L112) merges defaults, normalizes separators, copies `disabled_filetypes` into the statusline/winbar shape, and returns a deep copy of the active config.
3. Component/theme loading: [lua/lualine/utils/loader.lua](C:\Users\samch\AppData\Local\Temp\samchon-graph-corpus\lualine\lua\lualine\utils\loader.lua#L202) resets per-run state, then `load_components()` and `load_extensions()` turn config entries into live component objects. The dispatch logic that chooses module vs function vs vim expr is in [component_loader](C:\Users\samch\AppData\Local\Temp\samchon-graph-corpus\lualine\lua\lualine\utils\loader.lua#L59).
4. Theme/highlight creation: [lua/lualine/highlight.lua](C:\Users\samch\AppData\Local\Temp\samchon-graph-corpus\lualine\lua\lualine\highlight.lua#L200) converts the selected theme into concrete `lualine_*` highlight groups. `create_component_highlight_group()` at [line 305](C:\Users\samch\AppData\Local\Temp\samchon-graph-corpus\lualine\lua\lualine\highlight.lua#L305) is what component instances use to get their per-mode colors.
5. Render dispatch: [status_dispatch](C:\Users\samch\AppData\Local\Temp\samchon-graph-corpus\lualine\lua\lualine.lua#L290) chooses active vs inactive sections, skips disabled filetypes, and swaps in extension sections when a filetype matches. `tabline()` just reuses the same builder.
6. Section assembly: [lua/lualine/utils/section.lua](C:\Users\samch\AppData\Local\Temp\samchon-graph-corpus\lualine\lua\lualine\utils\section.lua#L18) loops components in a section, calls `component:draw()`, and strips or rewires separators when colors change.
7. Component contract: [lua/lualine/component.lua](C:\Users\samch\AppData\Local\Temp\samchon-graph-corpus\lualine\lua\lualine\component.lua#L271) is the base draw pipeline. `draw()` calls `update_status()` from the concrete component, then applies `fmt`, icons, padding, highlights, section separators, and the trailing separator. The base helpers are around [init](C:\Users\samch\AppData\Local\Temp\samchon-graph-corpus\lualine\lua\lualine\component.lua#L33), [apply_highlights](C:\Users\samch\AppData\Local\Temp\samchon-graph-corpus\lualine\lua\lualine\component.lua#L119), and [apply_section_separators](C:\Users\samch\AppData\Local\Temp\samchon-graph-corpus\lualine\lua\lualine\component.lua#L197).
8. Refresh loop: [refresh](C:\Users\samch\AppData\Local\Temp\samchon-graph-corpus\lualine\lua\lualine.lua#L360) batches updates, tracks the focused window in `vim.g.actual_curwin`, and writes the evaluated strings back via `nvim_opts`. The timers/autocmd setup for statusline/tabline/winbar lives in [set_statusline](C:\Users\samch\AppData\Local\Temp\samchon-graph-corpus\lualine\lua\lualine.lua#L533), [set_tabline](C:\Users\samch\AppData\Local\Temp\samchon-graph-corpus\lualine\lua\lualine.lua#L467), and [set_winbar](C:\Users\samch\AppData\Local\Temp\samchon-graph-corpus\lualine\lua\lualine.lua#L594).

**Concrete Component Paths**
1. Generic function component: [lua/lualine/components/special/function_component.lua](C:\Users\samch\AppData\Local\Temp\samchon-graph-corpus\lualine\lua\lualine\components\special\function_component.lua#L5) is the simplest example of the base contract.
2. Filename component: [lua/lualine/components/filename.lua](C:\Users\samch\AppData\Local\Temp\samchon-graph-corpus\lualine\lua\lualine\components\filename.lua#L72) shows real buffer-derived logic, path shortening, and modified/readonly flags.
3. Diagnostics component: [lua/lualine/components/diagnostics/init.lua](C:\Users\samch\AppData\Local\Temp\samchon-graph-corpus\lualine\lua\lualine\components\diagnostics\init.lua#L14) is a good “non-trivial” example with cached state, colored subparts, and insert-mode behavior.
4. Tabline components: [lua/lualine/components/tabs/init.lua](C:\Users\samch\AppData\Local\Temp\samchon-graph-corpus\lualine\lua\lualine\components\tabs\init.lua#L42), [lua/lualine/components/buffers/init.lua](C:\Users\samch\AppData\Local\Temp\samchon-graph-corpus\lualine\lua\lualine\components\buffers\init.lua#L51), and [lua/lualine/components/windows/init.lua](C:\Users\samch\AppData\Local\Temp\samchon-graph-corpus\lualine\lua\lualine\components\windows\init.lua#L9) are the main tabline-family implementations.

**What To Read Next**
1. [tests/spec/config_spec.lua](C:\Users\samch\AppData\Local\Temp\samchon-graph-corpus\lualine\tests\spec\config_spec.lua#L6) first, because it documents exactly how config defaults are normalized.
2. [tests/spec/component_spec.lua](C:\Users\samch\AppData\Local\Temp\samchon-graph-corpus\lualine\tests\spec\component_spec.lua#L12) next, because it exercises the base component lifecycle, separators, padding, icons, color handling, and `draw()`.
3. [tests/spec/utils_spec.lua](C:\Users\samch\AppData\Local\Temp\samchon-graph-corpus\lualine\tests\spec\utils_spec.lua#L100) for the section assembly and highlight helper behavior.
4. [tests/spec/lualine_spec.lua](C:\Users\samch\AppData\Local\Temp\samchon-graph-corpus\lualine\tests\spec\lualine_spec.lua#L85) for the end-to-end statusline path; then jump to [line 361](C:\Users\samch\AppData\Local\Temp\samchon-graph-corpus\lualine\tests\spec\lualine_spec.lua#L361) for extensions, [line 396](C:\Users\samch\AppData\Local\Temp\samchon-graph-corpus\lualine\tests\spec\lualine_spec.lua#L396) for tabline, [line 952](C:\Users\samch\AppData\Local\Temp\samchon-graph-corpus\lualine\tests\spec\lualine_spec.lua#L952) for diagnostics, and [line 1063](C:\Users\samch\AppData\Local\Temp\samchon-graph-corpus\lualine\tests\spec\lualine_spec.lua#L1063) for compound filetypes.
5. If you expect to change the redraw scheduler or window-focus behavior, also read [lua/lualine.lua](C:\Users\samch\AppData\Local\Temp\samchon-graph-corpus\lualine\lua\lualine.lua#L360) and [lua/lualine/utils/nvim_opts.lua](C:\Users\samch\AppData\Local\Temp\samchon-graph-corpus\lualine\lua\lualine\utils\nvim_opts.lua#L1); that is where the option writes and restore logic live.

One practical note: the test suite gives strong coverage of rendering output, but there is less direct unit coverage of the refresh queue/timer machinery itself. If your first behavior change touches scheduling rather than string rendering, I’d add a focused spec around that path.
